items.headline: Equities See Orderly Selloff Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Sector Rotation
items.summary: U.S. equity markets are experiencing an orderly selloff, with investors repositioning towards energy and commodities due to rising oil and natural gas prices. Large-cap growth stocks, particularly in semiconductors and software, are facing headwinds after becoming expensive. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, specifically the Iran conflict, remain a significant driver of market sentiment and inflation concerns.

items.headline: S&P 500
items.statContext: Down 0.61% for the session, lowest of 2026. Lost 1.6% for the week.

items.headline: NASDAQ Composite
items.statContext: Declined 0.93% for the session. Dropped 1.3% for the week.

items.headline: Dow Jones Industrial Average
items.statContext: Dropped 119.38 points for the session. Fell nearly 2% for the week.

items.headline: Sable Offshore Corp. Leads March Gainers
items.summary: Sable Offshore Corp. ($SOC) was a top performer in March, reflecting strong momentum.

items.headline: CATL Jumps on Earnings
items.summary: Hong Kong-listed battery maker CATL surged following a positive earnings report.

items.headline: Adobe Plunges on Weak Guidance
items.summary: Software giant Adobe experienced a sharp decline after missing guidance and announcing a CEO departure.

items.headline: Campbell's Falls on Q2 Results
items.summary: Campbell's ($CPB) dropped significantly following weak second-quarter fiscal 2026 results and reduced full-year guidance, largely due to its snacks segment.

items.headline: Fed Expected to Hold Rates Steady Next Week Amid Geopolitical Headwinds
items.summary: The U.S. Federal Reserve is widely anticipated to keep interest rates unchanged at its March 17-18 meeting, maintaining the federal funds rate at 3.5%-3.75%. Market attention will focus on updated economic projections and the 'dot plot,' which will likely incorporate the economic impact of the ongoing Iran conflict, surging oil prices, and potential new tariffs.

items.headline: Energy and Commodities Lead as Investors Seek Safety
items.summary: Investors are actively rotating into energy and commodity sectors, driven by escalating oil and natural gas prices amidst global tensions. Last week, energy, utilities, and select tech stocks were among the leading sectors, while large-cap growth, particularly in software and semiconductors, faced pressure.

items.headline: Key Economic Indicators Point to Weakness
items.summary: Recent economic data indicates underlying weakness, with U.S. GDP growth revised sharply lower in the final months of 2025. Inflation remains above the Fed's 2% target, with February CPI rising 2.4%. Concerns about dropping labor demand and slowing wage growth also persist.

items.headline: Earnings Reports: Surf Air Mobility and GoPro in Focus
items.summary: Surf Air Mobility ($SRFM) released Q4 earnings, reporting a 6% year-over-year revenue decline to $26.4 million, with 2026 full-year revenue guidance set between $128 million and $138 million. GoPro ($GPRO) also released earnings earlier in March, missing analyst estimates and providing Q1 2026 EPS guidance of -0.230 to -0.170.
